summary: Full end-to-end testing of Frontseat Seeding (backend + frontend). 22/22 pytest backend tests passed covering auth, role-scoping, admin approve, brief creation, fulfillment deliverable/output/note updates, payment update, page+user admin endpoints, and the critical fulfillment role-stripping check (price_closed_at and payment absent). Frontend playwright run validated Admin Overview (revenue=Snoball ₹3,20,000, OWLED Core ₹5,20,000), Approval Queue with action buttons, Users page (6 users with role/team dropdowns), Monetisable Pages (7 pages with active toggles), BD Snoball dashboard showing ONLY its own team's deals (Razorpay+Zoho+TEST, no Notion/CRED/Toothless), Submit Brief form, and Fulfillment dashboard with gallery/kanban/timeline view switchers showing only approved deals.

minor.issue: payment_pending_amount uses buggy expression `p.get('amount_received') is None and 0 or 0` which is always 0; field name implies it should sum outstanding/pending amounts.
Auth fallback via `?auth=` query param only verifies session row exists but does not actually attach the user context — may bypass active/role checks for file downloads.
allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will block credentialed cross-origin requests. Recommend listing exact origins.
Production-unsafe helper is unconditionally exposed; should be disabled by an env flag in non-preview environments.

integration_issues.issue: Default view did not surface the Zoho deal (Approved+Completed) — possibly filtered out by status. Backend correctly returns it. Confirm intended UX (hide completed by default vs show under timeline/completed tab).

action_items: Fix payment_pending_amount aggregation in /api/reports/overview to compute (deal price - amount_received) or similar.
Harden /api/files/{file_id} ?auth= fallback to actually resolve the user (active check + role).
Gate /api/auth/dev-session behind an env flag (e.g. ENABLE_DEV_SESSION) so it cannot be enabled in production.
Restrict CORS allow_origins to specific domains instead of '*' since allow_credentials=True.
Verify whether Fulfillment dashboard hiding Completed deals by default is intentional; if not, surface them under the timeline/kanban Completed column.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1140 lines and mixes auth, all CRUD, seed, reports, file storage. Recommend splitting into routers/{auth,deals,deliverables,reports,seed,files}.py and a shared models.py for maintainability.
Role scoping is enforced inline in get_deal/update_deal/update_payment/create_feedback. Consider a single dependency (e.g. get_deal_for_user) to centralize 403 checks and avoid drift.
payment_pending_amount logic is dead (always 0); needs correct aggregation.
All endpoints correctly exclude Mongo _id via projection — good.
data-testid coverage looks comprehensive on admin/BD pages (role-select-*, toggle-page-*, bd-deal-card-*, view-* switchers all found by Playwright).

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 tests, 1 skipped — pending-user direct DB scenario)

test_credentials: Dev session via POST /api/auth/dev-session with seeded emails (jaskaran.sethi@owledmedia.com, snoball.bd@owledmedia.com, hooc.bd@owledmedia.com, core.bd@owledmedia.com, ay.bd@owledmedia.com, om@owledmedia.com). Token usable as cookie session_token or Authorization: Bearer.
seed_data_creation: Auto-seed at startup created 4 teams, 7 pages, 6 users, 5 sample deals. Test run additionally created: (a) one TEST_Brand brief via Snoball BD, (b) approved the seeded Razorpay deal (admin_review_status now Approved), (c) updated Notion payment status to 'Payment Pending', (d) added a TEST_feedback, output, note, and updated one Notion deliverable to status=Designing/views=1234. State is no longer pristine — main agent should re-run /api/seed?force=true if pristine seed state is needed for re-verification, but functionality is unchanged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Razorpay is no longer in 'Submitted' state — it was approved during this test run. To retest the Approval Queue 'Approve' flow, either re-seed with force=true or create a fresh brief via /api/briefs then approve it. The TEST_Brand_xxx submitted brief from this run is still in the queue.
rca of the issue: No blocking bugs found. All listed minor issues are code-quality / hardening recommendations rather than functional defects.
